prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a sturdy stone foundation slab beneath a tall gleaming machine, tiny diligent worker characters laying down a strip of evenly spaced glowing dots into the slab while a single stern guardian character holds up an hourglass like a barrier, refusing to let a sneaky tendril of light from the future curl backward into the dots. caption: Every model in the book stands on this slab; lay the data honestly and even a humble model can be trusted, lay it carelessly and the smartest machine learns tomorrow and calls it skill.

after_heading: 2. Time Zones and the Daylight Saving Trap
prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: one single glowing event spark hovering over a globe, and three identical clock faces around the world each wearing the spark like a hat but each showing a different hour, while a puzzled detective character tries to line them up into one true order and one clock smugly springs its hands forward an hour on its own. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces, one clear visual gag, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A single event spark over a globe is wearing three different clock faces as hats, each reading a different hour, while a detective tries to reconcile them into one order and one clock cheekily jumps its hands forward by an hour.
caption: The same instant wears a different clock-hat in every zone, and daylight saving lets one of them lie about the hour; a single canonical clock is the only way to stop events from quietly reordering.

prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a tidy row of small calm grey dots, and right in the middle one conspicuous empty slot that is brightly glowing and pulsing as if it were the most important thing in the row, with a curious character leaning in fascinated by the glowing gap rather than the dots, gently lifting it like a gem. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive face, one clear idea, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A neat row of plain grey dots has one empty slot in the middle that glows brightly, and a curious character leans in treating the glowing gap as the most valuable item in the row, lifting it like a gem.
caption: A gap is not always noise to be filled; sometimes the very fact that a value is missing is the brightest signal in the row, which is why imputation should travel with a mask that remembers where the gap was.

after_heading: 1. Lag Features and the Autoregressive View
prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a relaxed contented character labelled by nothing strolling comfortably a few steps BEHIND a moving present-moment marker, always trailing it, calmly handing the marker a basket of things it already gathered in the past, while a reckless rival character ahead tries to grab from a forbidden glowing region in front and gets a gentle warning glow. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces, one clear visual gag, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A relaxed character strolls a few steps behind a moving present-moment marker, calmly handing it a basket of already-gathered past values, while a reckless rival reaching ahead into a glowing forbidden future region triggers a warning.
caption: An honest feature lives comfortably in the past and hands the present only what was already known; the instant it reaches forward for a value it should not have, it stops being a predictor and starts leaking.

alt: A rectangular frame glides along a long ribbon of wavy data, and at each position it stamps out a uniformly shaped card, building a tidy stack of cards beside the ribbon as an operator pushes the frame along like a stencil.
caption: Windowing is a frame that slides along the series and stamps a long continuous signal into a stack of fixed-shape supervised samples, the form every batch learner expects to be fed.

after_heading: 5. Backtest Realism: Costs, Drift, Multiple Testing, and Baselines
prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a smug robot lounging proudly beside a suspiciously perfect upward equity curve admiring its own reflection, while a sneaky disembodied hand slips it a glowing card from a region clearly marked as the future, the robot blissfully unaware it is cheating. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive face, one clear visual gag, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A smug robot lounges beside a flawless upward equity curve admiring itself, unaware that a sneaky hand is slipping it a glowing card drawn from the future, so its perfect track record is quietly the product of cheating.
caption: A backtest that looks far too good to be true usually is; somewhere a hand is slipping the model tomorrow's answer, and the flawless curve is the receipt for a leak rather than proof of skill.

prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a calm honest two-faced guardian character, one face looking forward and brightly lit showing a rich pile of everything known today, the other face looking back over its shoulder dimmer and showing a smaller humbler pile of only what was known back then, the character carefully handing a younger model character the smaller backward pile while keeping the bigger one behind its back. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces, one clear idea, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A calm two-faced guardian has one bright forward face holding everything known today and one dimmer backward-looking face holding only what was known earlier, and it hands a younger model the smaller backward pile while keeping the larger present-day pile out of reach.
caption: Point-in-time correctness means a pipeline serves a past moment only what that moment actually knew, honestly forgetting everything it has learned since, so a backtest can be rerun and trusted months later.
